//: Per-repo Tandem conventions (TEP-6). `acceptanceProbe` is this repo's held-out acceptance-probe recipe (mechanism 5, runnable half): how a *runnable* AC is graded by an INDEPENDENTLY-authored probe instead of the self-graded whole suite. Placeholders: `{spec}` = the SANITIZED Spec id — the composite `<tep>/<sp>` with every non-[A-Za-z0-9._-] run replaced by `_`, so spec `6/3` fills as `6_3` (one canonical rule, implemented by fillProbeTemplate; the TEP segment keeps probes from colliding across TEPs) — and `{ac}` = the 1-based AC ordinal. The methodology fills these templates; the LANGUAGE lives HERE, so the same methodology works against a Python repo (pytest), a Rust crate (cargo test), Ansible (molecule), etc. `sourcePath` is where the test-author writes the probe; `prepare` (optional) is the BUILD step the closing gate runs ONCE before the per-AC commands — needed when `run` targets compiled output, omitted for from-source runners like pytest; `run` is what the gate executes per AC. For this TypeScript extension: sources under src/ compile to out-test/ (tsconfig.test.json include: src/**/*), run by node:test.
//selfVerify: SP-12: the canonical, non-mutating build-and-test command a CODE-author worker runs to self-verify its edits — a PEER of `acceptanceProbe`, read by defaultAcceptanceRecipeResolver into AcceptanceRecipe.selfVerify and surfaced into the worker prompt's VERIFICATION BLOCK. It compiles the whole src tree to the GITIGNORED out-test/ (so it never surfaces to the footprint containment check — guard-safe by construction) and runs node:test over it. The worker no longer has to reverse-engineer this from tsconfig.test.json or improvise into shared build config.
//testExample: SP-16: a repo-relative path to a CANONICAL example test file — a PEER of `acceptanceProbe` / `selfVerify`, read by defaultAcceptanceRecipeResolver into AcceptanceRecipe.testExample (the file's CONTENT) and injected verbatim under the `EXAMPLE TEST` marker into every role:test worker prompt. It shows the repo's fixture-construction + assertion idiom ONCE so a held-out test worker writes its probe straight from prompt + contract instead of independently rediscovering the idiom (reading whole files, hitting the 25k Read cap). Chosen for being right-sized and self-contained: node:test + node:assert/strict, a fixture-stub factory (`fakeQuery`), shared fixture constants (`ACS`), and substring/deep-equal assertions — the same idiom a probe reproduces. Declaration-driven and repo-agnostic: a Python/Rust/Ansible repo points this at its own example test; nothing is hardcoded in the orchestrator.

acceptanceProbe.prepare: rm -rf out-test/acceptance && npx tsc -p tsconfig.test.json && npx tsc -p ./ && node scripts/build-assets.mjs && node esbuild.mcp.mjs

//host: TEP-21/SP-2 (subject fidelity): `run` is now an ALTITUDE DISPATCHER. A component-level AC's probe is authored at sourcePath (node:test); a SURFACE-level AC's probe is authored as src/acceptance/SP-{spec}_AC-{ac}.host.ts and runs inside a REAL VS Code extension host (xvfb + @vscode/test-electron via out-test/test/runAcceptanceHost.js; canonical example: src/harness/harnessSmoke.host.ts). A host probe declaring `// TANDEM_PHASES=2` gets one FRESH host per phase (resume-across-restart). `prepare` also builds dist/ + assets so the host can load the development extension.
